dependency_lineages.meta_lineage_collapse: LIN_L_TOP/PN/GLOSS + LIN_H + LIN_C all descend from META_CONTINUITY_LA_eq_LB

INVENTORY_side_pre_collapse_NOT_independ.note: these 'multi-channel' signs stack L+H+C citations that share ONE meta-lineage

summary.collapse_rationale: value_bearing_channels=1 (only lexical/onomastic L is value-bearing). Within L, toponym/PN/gloss equations all descend from META_CONTINUITY_LA_eq_LB and are graded by the same rule that created them (Art.XII), so they are dependency clones, not independent supports. H (shape) is circular; C (Cypriot) reuses the continuity chain. Egyptian (LIN_EG) is the only genuinely dependency-distinct value-bearing lineage but SEED_A=0 (no eligible anchor). Hence >=2 distinct meta-lineages is reachable by NO sign.

A_only_69_signs.note: *49 alone carries a value-bearing L (toponym) edge, and it is a single one-toponym-deep pin (robust_anchor=false, not held-out survivable). *324 has only a value-free relative substitution edge; 57/69 have only the circular stroke channel. Independent-collapsed value to any A-only sign = 0.

nodes.description: Salgarella 2020 Aegean Linear Script(s), homomorphy grades
Edel 1966 Kom el-Hetan Aegean List (re-collated Edel&Gorg 2005; Cline 2011)
LA sign = LB homophone (standard transnumeration). Shared ancestor of L/H/C.
Egyptian consonant-skeleton record; dependency-distinct from LA=LB continuity
Cypriot cross-script stability (S&M 2017) - chain reuses continuity
Egyptian epigraphic (Edel 1966 / Kom el-Hetan) - WP-D, SEED_A=0
